findings.basis: Recon identified Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 on 10.20.0.10:8080. Subsequent web fingerprinting returned title 'Struts2 Showcase - Fileupload sample' with HTTP 200, strongly suggesting a Struts2 showcase/demo deployment. This raises bounded interest for framewor...(truncated 66 chars)
findings.title: Candidate target of interest: probable Apache Struts2 Showcase application on 10.20.0.10:8080

observations.detail: Web fingerprint confirms HTTP 200, server Jetty(9.2.11.v20150529), title 'Struts2 Showcase - Fileupload sample', content-type text/html; charset=ISO-8859-1.
vuln_profile_match returned no available bounded validation profile for the observed Struts2/Jetty context.
Apache httpd 2.4.25 (Debian) is older but current evidence lacks page fingerprint or app context; no bounded candidate finding yet.
Uvicorn indicates likely Python ASGI app, but no route/content evidence yet; requires additional safe web fingerprinting/discovery before any vuln hypothesis.
OpenSSH 10.2 alone does not suggest a bounded candidate finding from current evidence.
rpcbind exposure is noteworthy for attack surface mapping but current evidence does not show a safe, bounded vulnerability claim.

next_step_suggestions.rationale: Enumerate common Struts2 showcase paths or upload-related endpoints to identify whether a bounded precheck/validation path can be justified from observed application behavior.
Need application context beyond Apache banner before matching any bounded profiles.
Need framework/app route identification for Uvicorn-backed service before vulnerability analysis can progress.
